name: Maryknoll of L a
description_short: Maryknoll Shotokan Karate Club is a 60-year-old nonprofit karate dojo in Los Angeles offering traditional martial arts training, not consumer finance services.
description_long:
Maryknoll Shotokan Karate Club was founded in 1963 by Tsutomu Ohshima, a Japanese language teacher at Maryknoll Japanese Catholic School in downtown Los Angeles. The club originated when an eighth-grade student convinced Mr. Ohshima to teach karate, making it the first elementary school karate club in the United States. Mr. Ohshima was a direct student of Master Gichin Funakoshi, the founder of modern karate, and went on to establish Shotokan Karate of America (SKA), a nonprofit organization dedicated to teaching traditional karate throughout the United States since 1956.

Maryknoll Shotokan Karate offers rigorous traditional karate training for students of all levels. Classes are held three times per week at St. Francis Xavier Chapel Japanese Catholic Center in Little Tokyo, Los Angeles, with practice sessions on Tuesday and Thursday evenings (7:30-9pm) and Saturday mornings (8:30-10am). The instruction follows the form and tradition passed down directly from Master Funakoshi through Mr. Ohshima to the current senior black belt instructors.

What distinguishes Maryknoll is its historical significance and lineage. As one of the oldest continuously operating karate dojos in the United States, it celebrated its 60th anniversary in 2023 and remains one of the original Shotokan schools established by Mr. Ohshima. The club maintains direct connection to the traditional teaching methods and philosophy of Master Gichin Funakoshi, the founder of modern karate.

This company is not a consumer finance service and has been miscategorized. It is a nonprofit martial arts organization offering karate instruction to the Los Angeles community. There are no financial products, loans, credit services, or consumer finance offerings of any kind. The profile should be removed from CreditDoc as it falls outside the scope of consumer finance research.
